chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT HYDERABAD C.P. No.S-1850 of 2016 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on MA-16522/2016 2. For orders on office objection 3. For katcha peshi. 4. For orders on MA-16523/2016 16.11.2016. Ms. Shabana Noreen Khan, Advocate alongwith petitioner. = 1. Granted. 2. Deferred for the time being. 3&4. It is, inter-alia, contended that son of the petitioner Ali Raza has contracted a freewill marriage with Seema, who is daughter of respondent No.4-Syed Ali Hassan, which has antagonized the private respondents and they are extending threats to the petitioner. Record shows that earlier the petitioner has approached the learned Sessions Judge/Ex-Officio Justice of Peace, Shaheed Benazirabad by filing Cr.Misc.Application No.1457/2016, which was disposed of by order dated 03.11.2016 by issuing directions that official respondents should conduct themselves in accordance with law. As per the learned Counsel, the official respondents are not acting in accordance with law and are not providing any protection to the petitioner against the harassment being caused by the private respondents. Issue notice to the private respondents by all modes except publication as well as official respondents and A.A.G for 07.12.2016. On the next date of hearing both the concerned SHOs of P.S Sakran and Mari Jalbani of Shaheed Benazirabad are directed to ensure the presence of respondents No.4, 5 & 7 namely Syed Ali Hassan, Hussain Shah and Subhan Ali @ Gulshan Ali respectively before this Court. In the meantime, private respondents are restrained from extending any threats or causing any harassment to the petitioner and official respondents are further directed to provide protection to the petitioner, if they are approached with such a request.

On the next date of hearing both the concerned SHOs of P.S Sakran and Mari Jalbani of Shaheed Benazirabad are directed to ensure the presence of respondents No.4, 5 & 7 namely Syed Ali Hassan, Hussain Shah and Subhan Ali @ Gulshan Ali respectively before this Court. In the meantime, private respondents are restrained from extending any threats or causing any harassment to the petitioner and official respondents are further directed to provide protection to the petitioner, if they are approached with such a request. JUDGE Shahid
